# products - SEO + GEO Remediation Playbook

**Target**: https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
**Backing service**: aicart/intelliverse-products (image `intelliverse-products:latest`)
**Source repo (best guess)**: intelli-verse-x/intelliverse-products
**Audit reference**: `reports/products.md`
**Generated**: 2026-05-17T18:20:00Z
**Generated by**: gastown audits/crew/auditor (firecrawl + claude)

---

## 1. Executive summary

The products subdomain returns HTTP 404 with an empty body—DNS resolves and TLS connects, but no application is deployed or the backend is misconfigured. This is a critical SEO/GEO failure: search engines will mark it as a dead link, and AI systems have zero information about the service. The headline fix depends on intent: (a) if the site should be live, deploy the application and add full SEO metadata; (b) if under construction, serve a "Coming Soon" page with noindex; (c) if not needed, redirect to the main domain or remove DNS. Projected impact: 0/10 → 6-8/10 LLM-friendliness once content is live; or 0/10 → 3/10 if "Coming Soon" page is served.

From `reports/products.md`, all checks FAIL due to HTTP 404:

- ✗ **HTTP accessibility** ([report#L10](reports/products.md#L10)): Returns HTTP 404 with empty body (CRITICAL)
- ✗ **title tag** ([report#L11](reports/products.md#L11)): N/A — no HTML returned

- ✗ **sitemap.xml** ([report#L15](reports/products.md#L15)): N/A — cannot audit
- ✗ **HSTS** ([report#L16](reports/products.md#L16)): N/A — no response headers visible
- ✗ **Open Graph** ([report#L17](reports/products.md#L17)): N/A — no HTML returned
- ✗ **Twitter Card** ([report#L18](reports/products.md#L18)): N/A — no HTML returned

- ✗ **lang attribute** ([report#L21](reports/products.md#L21)): N/A — no HTML returned
- ✗ **CSP** ([report#L22](reports/products.md#L22)): N/A — no response headers visible

**GEO findings** ([report#L24-L37](reports/products.md#L24-L37)): AI-answerability FAIL (0/10). The subdomain is non-functional; status suggests either under construction, misconfigured, or abandoned.

## 3. 2026 best-practice baseline (researched via firecrawl)

### SEO fundamentals

**Source**: [Google Search Central - SEO Starter Guide](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/seo-starter-guide) (2026)

> "Help Google find your content... pages must return HTTP 200 with crawlable HTML."

**Key requirement**: HTTP 404 pages are NOT indexed. If a subdomain exists in DNS but returns 404, search engines treat it as a broken link.

**Custom 404 pages**: Google recommends returning a helpful 404 page with navigation back to working content, but these are still not indexed.

### Crawler control

**Source**: [Google Search Central - robots meta tag](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/robots-meta-tag)

For pages under construction: `<meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow">` prevents indexing until ready.

### Structured data

**Source**: [Schema.org - Product](https://schema.org/Product) (2026)

For product catalog sites, use `Product` or `ItemList` schemas:
- `@type: "Product"` with `name`, `description`, `offers`, `image`
- Or `@type: "ItemList"` for catalog pages

### Security signals

Same headers as ai-host remediation: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP are 2026 baseline.

### GEO/LLM-readability

**Source**: [llms.txt specification](https://llmstxt.org) (2026)

Product catalog sites benefit from `/llms.txt` listing key product categories and markdown docs for each.

## 4. Concrete remediation (prioritized)

### Scenario A: Site should be active

### 1. Deploy the products application

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 4-8 hours (depends on root cause)
- **What**: Deploy the intelliverse-products application to the web server
- **Why**: Fixes HTTP 404 ([report#L10](reports/products.md#L10)), enables all subsequent SEO/GEO work
- **Where**: Check `intelli-verse-x/intelliverse-products` repo for deployment issues
- **How**: Diagnose and fix deployment:

```bash
# 1. Check if Docker image exists
docker pull intelliverse-products:latest

# 2. Verify Kubernetes deployment
kubectl get pods -n aicart | grep intelliverse-products
kubectl logs -n aicart deployment/intelliverse-products

# 3. Check Ingress routing
kubectl get ingress -n aicart -o yaml | grep products.intelli-verse-x.ai

# 4. Verify Service endpoint
kubectl get svc -n aicart intelliverse-products

# Common fixes:
# - If deployment scaled to 0: kubectl scale deployment/intelliverse-products --replicas=1 -n aicart
# - If image pull failed: check image registry, update image tag
# - If Ingress misconfigured: verify host routing, backend service name
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -I https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 200 (not 404)
```

### 2. Add SEO metadata to products application

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 2-4 hours
- **What**: Add title, meta description, OG tags, JSON-LD to product pages
- **Why**: Enables indexing and social sharing once app is deployed
- **Where**: Likely `intelliverse-products/src/index.html` or `app/layout.tsx` (depends on stack)
- **How** (example for Next.js):

```tsx
// intelliverse-products/app/layout.tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: 'Products | IntelliVerse X - AI-Powered Solutions',
  description: 'Explore IntelliVerse X product catalog: AI chatbots, tutoring systems, quiz platforms, and more. Discover intelligent solutions for education and business.',
  openGraph: {
    title: 'Products | IntelliVerse X',
    description: 'AI-powered products and solutions',
    url: 'https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai',
    siteName: 'IntelliVerse X',
    type: 'website',
  },
  twitter: {
    card: 'summary_large_image',
    title: 'Products | IntelliVerse X',
    description: 'AI-powered products and solutions',
  },
};
```

Or for static HTML:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Products | IntelliVerse X - AI-Powered Solutions</title>
    <meta name="description" content="Explore IntelliVerse X product catalog: AI chatbots, tutoring systems, quiz platforms, and more.">
    
    <link rel="canonical" href="https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <meta property="og:title" content="Products | IntelliVerse X">
    <meta property="og:description" content="AI-powered products and solutions">
    <meta property="og:type" content="website">
    <meta property="og:url" content="https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
    <meta name="twitter:title" content="Products | IntelliVerse X">
    
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
      "@context": "https://schema.org",
      "@type": "ItemList",
      "name": "IntelliVerse X Products",
      "description": "AI-powered products and solutions",
      "itemListElement": []
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
    <main>
        <h1>IntelliVerse X Products</h1>
        <!-- product catalog content -->
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E '<title>|<meta name="description"|application/ld\+json'
```

### 3. Add robots.txt and sitemap.xml

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Create crawler control files
- **Why**: Fixes missing crawler control ([report#L14-L15](reports/products.md#L14-L15))
- **Where**: `intelliverse-products/public/robots.txt` and `public/sitemap.xml`
- **How**:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Allow: /

User-agent: Claude-Web
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
```

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>weekly</changefreq>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
  <!-- Add product pages as they're created -->
</urlset>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt | grep "Sitemap:"
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | grep "<?xml"
```

### 4. Add Product schema to individual product pages

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 2-4 hours
- **What**: Add JSON-LD `Product` schema to each product detail page
- **Why**: Enables rich snippets in search results, improves GEO answerability
- **Where**: Product detail page template (e.g., `products/[id].tsx`)
- **How**:

```html
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Product",
  "name": "AI Tutor Platform",
  "description": "Intelligent tutoring system powered by AI for personalized education",
  "image": "https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/images/ai-tutor.jpg",
  "brand": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "IntelliVerse X"
  },
  "offers": {
    "@type": "Offer",
    "url": "https://tutor.intelli-verse-x.ai",
    "price": "0",
    "priceCurrency": "USD",
    "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock"
  }
}
</script>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/some-product | grep -c '"@type": "Product"'
```

### 5. Add security headers

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Configure HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP
- **Why**: Fixes missing security signals ([report#L16,L22](reports/products.md#L16)), improves SEO ranking
- **Where**: Nginx config or application middleware
- **How** (Nginx):

```nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "DENY" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "no-referrer-when-downgrade" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';" always;
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sSI https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -iE 'strict-transport|x-content-type'
```

### 6. Add llms.txt for GEO

- **Priority**: P2 — estimated 1 hour
- **What**: Create `/llms.txt` with product catalog overview
- **Why**: Improves GEO answerability for AI assistants
- **Where**: `intelliverse-products/public/llms.txt`
- **How**:

```markdown
# IntelliVerse X Products

> A catalog of AI-powered products and solutions including chatbots, tutoring systems, quiz platforms, animation tools, and business solutions.

Product categories:

- **Education**: AI Tutor, Quiz Platform, QuizX (advanced quizzes)
- **Content Creation**: Animator (AI video generation), Blogs platform
- **Business Tools**: MarketX (marketplace), Kiosk, Platform (user management)
- **AI Chat**: AI Chat v2 (conversational AI), AI Host (real-time WebSocket chat)

## Products

- [AI Tutor](https://tutor.intelli-verse-x.ai): Intelligent tutoring system for personalized education
- [Quiz Platform](https://quiz.intelli-verse-x.ai): Interactive quiz and assessment tools
- [Animator](https://animator.intelli-verse-x.ai): AI-powered video and animation generation
- [MarketX](https://marketx.intelli-verse-x.ai): Marketplace platform for digital products
- [AI Chat v2](https://ai-chat-v2.intelli-verse-x.ai): Advanced conversational AI chatbot

## Documentation

- [Main site](https://intelli-verse-x.ai): IntelliVerse X ecosystem overview
- [Platform](https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai): User management and authentication
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt | head -20
```

---

### Scenario B: Site is under construction

If the products site is not ready to launch, serve a "Coming Soon" page:

### 1. Create "Coming Soon" page

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 1 hour
- **What**: Deploy a simple HTML holding page with noindex meta tag
- **Why**: Prevents dead 404 link, provides context to users
- **Where**: Create `intelliverse-products/public/index.html` or static nginx page
- **How**:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Products | Coming Soon - IntelliVerse X</title>
    <meta name="description" content="IntelliVerse X product catalog - launching soon">
    <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow">
    <link rel="canonical" href="https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: center;
            min-height: 100vh;
            margin: 0;
            background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
            color: white;
            text-align: center;
        }
        main { padding: 2rem; }
        h1 { font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; }
        a {
            display: inline-block;
            margin-top: 2rem;
            padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;
            background: white;
            color: #667eea;
            text-decoration: none;
            border-radius: 4px;
            font-weight: 600;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <main>
        <h1>Products Catalog - Coming Soon</h1>
        <p>We're building an amazing product catalog. Check back soon!</p>
        <a href="https://intelli-verse-x.ai">Visit IntelliVerse X Home</a>
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

Deploy via:

```bash
# Option 1: Static nginx serve
kubectl create configmap products-holding-page --from-file=index.html=/path/to/above.html -n aicart
# Update deployment to mount ConfigMap and serve with nginx

# Option 2: Update existing deployment to serve this page
# Add to repo, rebuild image, deploy
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -I https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 200 (not 404)

curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep "Coming Soon"
```

---

### Scenario C: Site is not needed

If products.intelli-verse-x.ai should not exist:

### 1. Redirect to main domain

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Set up 301 redirect to main domain or products section
- **Why**: Prevents dead link, consolidates traffic
- **Where**: Nginx config or Ingress rule
- **How** (Nginx):

```nginx
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name products.intelli-verse-x.ai;
    
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/products.intelli-verse-x.ai/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/products.intelli-verse-x.ai/privkey.pem;
    
    return 301 https://intelli-verse-x.ai/products$request_uri;
}
```

Or Kubernetes Ingress:

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: products-redirect
  namespace: aicart
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/permanent-redirect: https://intelli-verse-x.ai/products
spec:
  rules:
  - host: products.intelli-verse-x.ai
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: redirect-service
            port:
              number: 80
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -I https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 301, Location: https://intelli-verse-x.ai/products
```

### 2. Remove DNS (alternative)

If no /products section exists on main site:

```bash
# Remove DNS A/AAAA record for products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Prevents users from reaching a 404 entirely
```

## 5. Stack-aware notes

Based on the service name (`intelliverse-products`) and k8s namespace, this is likely a **React/Vue SPA** or **Next.js app**.

**Key patterns**:

1. **Check if deployment exists**:
   ```bash
   kubectl get deployment intelliverse-products -n aicart
   ```
   If it doesn't exist, the subdomain was prematurely DNS-configured.

2. **React/Vue SPA**: If this is a client-side rendered app, ensure:
   - Build process runs correctly (`npm run build`)
   - Nginx serves `index.html` for all routes (SPA fallback)
   - HTML shell has proper `<title>` and `<meta>` tags

3. **Next.js**: Use `metadata` export in `app/layout.tsx` for SEO. Generate sitemap with `next-sitemap` package.

4. **Product schema**: If this is a catalog with many products, generate JSON-LD programmatically from product data, not hardcoded.

## 6. Verification plan

After deploy (Scenario A - site active):

```bash
# 1. HTTP accessibility
curl -I https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 200

# 2. SEO metadata
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E '<title>|<meta name="description"'
# Expected: 2 matches

# 3. Security headers
curl -sSI https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -iE 'strict-transport|x-content-type'
# Expected: 2+ matches

# 4. Structured data
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -c 'application/ld\+json'
# Expected: >= 1

# 5. Crawler files
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt

# 6. GEO answerability
firecrawl scrape https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-main-content -o /tmp/products-post.md
wc -l /tmp/products-post.md
# Expected: > 50 lines

# 7. Lighthouse audit
lighthouse https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-categories=seo,best-practices --output=json
# Target: SEO >= 90, Best Practices >= 90
```

After deploy (Scenario B - coming soon page):

```bash
curl -I https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 200

curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep "Coming Soon"
# Expected: match

curl -sS https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep 'name="robots"'
# Expected: content="noindex, nofollow"
```

After deploy (Scenario C - redirect):

```bash
curl -I https://products.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 301, Location: https://intelli-verse-x.ai/products
```

## 7. Open questions / blockers

1. **Which scenario applies?** — Need to confirm with product/engineering team:
   - Is products.intelli-verse-x.ai meant to be a live catalog? (Scenario A)
   - Is it under construction? (Scenario B)
   - Should it redirect or be removed? (Scenario C)

2. **Does the intelliverse-products repo exist?** — Need access to verify stack (React, Next.js, Vue?) and deployment configuration.

3. **Is there product data?** — If Scenario A applies, where is the product catalog data stored? (CMS, database, hardcoded JSON?)

4. **Who manages DNS?** — If Scenario C applies (remove DNS), confirm who has access to DNS provider (CloudFlare, Route53, etc.)

5. **Is there a /products section on the main site?** — If Scenario C redirect applies, verify https://intelli-verse-x.ai/products exists. If not, redirect to just https://intelli-verse-x.ai.

## 8. Estimated impact

### Scenario A (site goes live with full SEO)

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — HTTP 404, not indexed
- **After**: 7-8/10 — indexed catalog with product schemas
- **Improvement**: Site transitions from "dead link" to "indexed product catalog"
- **Indexing timeline**: 3-7 days after sitemap submission

**GEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — no information available
- **After**: 7-8/10 — LLMs can list products, describe features
- **Improvement**: AI assistants can answer "What products does IntelliVerse X offer?"
- **Indexing timeline**: 4-8 weeks (LLM crawler frequency)

**Projected traffic**:
- **Before**: 0 visitors (404 page)
- **After**: 100-300 organic visitors/month within 3 months (assuming product catalog has 10+ items)

### Scenario B (coming soon page)

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — HTTP 404
- **After**: 3/10 — HTTP 200 but noindex (not in search results)
- **Improvement**: Users get context instead of blank 404, but no SEO benefit

**GEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10
- **After**: 2/10 — LLMs know site exists but has no products yet

**Projected traffic**:
- **Before**: 0 visitors
- **After**: ~5-10 visitors/month (only direct/referral, no organic search)

### Scenario C (redirect to main domain)

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — HTTP 404 (negative signal)
- **After**: 5/10 — 301 redirect (consolidates to main domain, no standalone ranking)
- **Improvement**: Removes dead link penalty, funnels traffic to main site

**GEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10
- **After**: 0/10 (subdomain no longer exists independently)

**Projected traffic**:
- Subdomain traffic redirected to main domain; no standalone metrics
